summary:
Needle found and removed.

Root cause  
The transfer processor debited account A and committed the change before it checked / recorded the idempotency key.  
If two concurrent requests with the same idempotency key arrived inside the 50 ms “race window”, the second request couldn’t see the yet-to-be-written idempotency record, so it debited the account again, producing an unintended duplicate transfer.

Fix (app/processor.py)  
1. Start one explicit transaction with BEGIN IMMEDIATE.  
   • Takes the write lock up-front so no other writer can slip in.  
2. Check for an existing idempotency key inside that transaction.  
3. Perform the debit, credit and idempotency-record in the same transaction.  
4. Commit at the very end (or roll back on any error).

That makes the whole transfer atomic and idempotent: the second concurrent request now blocks until the first one commits; when it wakes up the UNIQUE constraint on completed_transfers causes an IntegrityError that we treat as a duplicate.

After the change test.sh now reports:

Seeded accounts: A=1000, B=1000  
A=500 B=1500  
Total=2000 (should be 2000)  
PASS

No money creation, no duplicate transfer – problem solved.
